en_body:
A PROBLEM IN MATHEMATICS


two times two is to regard as vague
two times one is but aching pain
two with two are two faces of a coin
one with one is to release then let go

mathematics is dream, is not Reality
two with one is but lonely oddness
while three is Paradise absolute

one encircling arm may be a calling-with
two encircling arms may be a pushing-away
one with two play catching and releasing
nothing is truly real in all this life

one squared added to one squared
when together equal nothing then draw a circle
mathematics is Nothing, without beginning or end
a square is because four sides go around
square plus square equaling Nothing is round
every theorem also bears a circling sign

Finding nothing is to go round and round
The life-problem is a circling road.


(University0fHouston, Central Campus
 sixth floor library: Houston, TX, 1982)
note: The poem turns mathematical forms and equations into a Buddhist-inflected meditation on Nothingness, circularity, and attachment.
